2026-05-01.label: Climb #1 — Auburn Quarry (substituted for Friday mobility)
2026-05-01.reason: Fri: traded mobility for a quarry climb (rope solo, climbed to forearm fatigue + took a fall). E-bike approach z2 w/ 20 lb pack, brief level-up on first 400 ft of stagecoach. Shoulder green throughout. There was no Monday climb this week (program started Tue 4/28), so net climb count for the week is unchanged. Saturday Long Day (conversational hike) stands.

log.did: Strength A; tested TGU at 20 lb — too heavy, dropping back to 17.5
log.notes: Post-workout ~3/10 anterior + superior left shoulder — confirmed average / baseline (not a flare). 20 lb TGU test failed; hold at 17.5, retest in 2 weeks. AM next-day check: 0–1/10, much better.
1/10 anterior shoulder burning during run, very mild. AM next-day check (4/30): 1/10, felt good overall.

log.did: Strength B as prescribed. Prone Y-T-W @ 5 lb DBs; Cossack squat @ 17.5 lb KB; ATG/Bulgarian split squat @ 15 lb KB; single-leg RDL @ 17.5 lb KB.
log.notes: Loads above week-1 prescription on Cossack (17.5 KB vs BW) and split squat (15 KB vs BW); single-leg RDL 17.5 vs prescribed 20. Workout went well. AM next-day check (5/1): zero pain.

log.did: Climb #1 — Auburn Quarry rope solo (substituted for Friday mobility). E-bike approach + return at zone 2 with 20 lb climbing pack; brief level-up for first 400 ft of stagecoach trail then back to z2 the rest of the way home. Climbed until forearm fatigue and took a fall. AM mobility stretches done.
log.notes: Zero shoulder pain throughout. Effectively shifted Monday's climbing slot to Friday — there was no Monday climb this week (program started Tue 4/28), so weekly climb count unchanged. Good leg burn from yesterday's Strength B. Garmin: MTB 6.5 mi/35:25/HR 132/348 m gain/load 72; climb 1:12:13/HR 118/load 52. Total Friday load ~124.

log.planned: Long day — Week A hike (long hilly hike, 2–4 hr conversational, 5/15/25 lb pack)
log.did: Trail run 6.3 mi, 1:11:14, avg HR 149, 259 m gain (Garmin) — substituted run for the prescribed hike. No self-report yet — fields below inferred from Garmin only.
log.notes: Inferred from Garmin pull on 5/3 — Erik hasn't reported this session yet. Ran instead of hiked: pace ~11:18/mi over 6.3 mi with 259 m gain = clearly running, not fast hiking. Avg HR 149 sits in upper z2 / low z3, similar to his 4/29 trail run (HR 156). Load 129 — meaningful aerobic load. Total week captured load: Wed 175 + Fri 124 + Sat 129 = 428.

2026-05-01.why_today: Friday is the deliberate offload between yesterday's Strength B and tomorrow's Long Day — the lightest slot of the week, by design. Mobility, not nothing.
2026-05-01.evidence: House & Johnston (Training for the New Alpinism) treat recovery days as part of the training, not a break from it: the body adapts to the loads it can recover from. Skipping the easy day degrades both the hard day before and the long day after, and is the classic path into the no-man's-land of moderate fatigue without adaptation.
2026-05-01.building_toward: Phase 1 base + shoulder. Mobility today keeps the runner anti-tightening work and the PT shoulder staples (band ER, foam roller t-spine) in weekly rotation — small consistent deposits, which House & Johnston identify as the actual driver of long-arc fitness over heroic single sessions.
2026-05-01.modification: Substituted Friday mobility for Climb #1 at Auburn Quarry. Garmin actuals: MTB approach 6.5 mi/35:25/avg HR 132/348 m gain/load 72 + rope solo climb 1:12:13/avg HR 118/load 52 = ~124 total. Three signals worth naming. (1) HR 132 on the e-bike with a 20 lb pack over 348 m of climbing is clean zone 2 — the brief stagecoach level-up didn't kick you out of aerobic, which is exactly what 'bike-as-warmup' is supposed to look like. (2) HR 118 average on the climb is conservative for a rope solo to forearm fatigue. The climb_quarry prescription says 'stop while you still have one good lap left' — your HR profile confirms you did, since max-effort cranking pushes climbing HR much higher than 118. (3) Shoulder green through both pieces is the PT-progression signal we want — a real climbing day at moderate intensity tolerated without flare is the data point that earns Phase 1's eventual unlock to Week B. Net read: the substitution served the program. Per the schedule, the bike ride IS the climb day warmup, so this is just Climb #1 landing on Friday — no Monday climb this week (program started Tue 4/28), so weekly climb count is unchanged. Week-to-date captured load: ~299 (Wed run 175 + today 124; Strength A/B don't get Garmin loads). ACWR not in spike range. Saturday Long Day stands but trimmed — see tomorrow's brief for the cascade.

2026-05-02.why_today: Saturday is the long-day slot — the week's highest volume, placed after Friday's offload so you come in fresh. Week A is a long hilly hike at conversational pace.
2026-05-02.evidence: This is the cornerstone session of the House & Johnston framework: long, low-intensity aerobic work at MAF / nose-breathing pace, building mitochondrial density and connective-tissue durability. They argue the aerobic base is the floor everything else (climbing endurance, recovery between hard efforts, day-after capacity) sits on — and that Zone 3 'tempo' work would actively undermine it.
2026-05-02.building_toward: Aerobic floor for the December marathon goal and the multi-pitch granite endurance you'll need in Yosemite. Per the Phase 1 stop-light: 4 symptom-free weeks unlocks Week B (tension board + easy run) and a second climbing day — the hike weeks are earning that transition.
2026-05-02.modification: Substituted a trail run for the prescribed hike (Garmin: 6.3 mi, 1:11:14, avg HR 149, 259 m gain, load 129). Three reads on this. (1) Intensity went the wrong direction. The Friday cascade said 'religious about conversational pace / nose-breathing — don't let any Z3 creep into the climbs.' HR 149 on a 6.3 mi run is upper z2 / low z3 — basically the same intensity profile as the 4/29 trail run (HR 156). That violates the H&J Long Day prescription, which is specifically about low-intensity aerobic volume; running at 149 BPM converts the Long Day from base-building into a moderate tempo session. (2) Volume came in lighter than the hike would have been. 1:11:14 vs the prescribed 2–4 hr means the connective-tissue / mitochondrial benefit of the Long Day was *not* delivered — even at 2 hr conservative, that's longer than 1:11. So you got intensity you didn't need and skipped the duration you did. (3) Total week captured load: 428 (Wed 175 + Fri 124 + Sat 129) — fine on ACWR, but mis-distributed. Net read: probably the legs felt cooked from Friday and the run was the path of least resistance at the actual capacity you had — if so, the fatigue was real and the right call was the shorter-duration hike, not a faster run. Worth asking Erik what drove the swap so we can either adjust the rule (Long Day defaults to a shorter, less z2-strict option after a Friday quarry stack) or reinforce it.
